Definition: Supported by formal clinical practice guidelines, Level I evidence, or strong consensus from major professional societies (e.g., APA, CANMAT, NICE). Typically based on multiple high-quality RCTs or meta-analyses.
Minimum_Source_Standard: Published clinical guideline or Level I systematic review/meta-analysis from recognized body
Notes: Verify device-specific indication and contraindications even for guideline-supported protocols. Guideline support does not override regulatory status.

Definition: Supported by published peer-reviewed studies including RCTs, controlled trials, or high-quality meta-analyses, but not yet incorporated into formal clinical guidelines. Evidence is promising but may have limitations in sample size, blinding, or replication.
Minimum_Source_Standard: Peer-reviewed RCT, controlled trial, or systematic review in indexed journal (PubMed-listed)
Notes: Patient-facing export requires clinician approval. Must cite specific studies. Note if evidence is from single-site or small-sample studies.

Definition: Preliminary or mixed evidence base. May include pilot studies, open-label trials, case series, or early-phase results. Evidence may show promise but lacks sufficient replication, blinding quality, or sample size for clinical confidence.
Minimum_Source_Standard: Published pilot study, open-label trial, or case series in peer-reviewed journal
Notes: Not for patient-facing export. Must be flagged as emerging in all outputs. Clinician review mandatory before any protocol activation.

Definition: Insufficient evidence for routine clinical use. May include preclinical data, single case reports, theoretical rationale only, or studies with significant methodological concerns. Includes novel combinations or off-label applications without supporting literature.
Minimum_Source_Standard: Any published source or theoretical rationale documented by reviewing clinician
Notes: Internal or research use only. Cannot be exported to patients. Cannot be used for treatment suggestions without explicit clinician authorization and IRB/ethics consideration where applicable.
